text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence designed to understand and generate human-like text. They work by analyzing patterns in language, using vast amounts of data from books, websites, and other written sources. Training data is crucial, as the model learns grammar, facts, and even conversational styles from this information. To use an LLM effectively, you rely on prompt engineering, which involves crafting clear questions or instructions to guide its responses. While these models can write essays, answer questions, or create stories, they have limits: they may produce incorrect facts or lack true understanding, so you should always verify their outputs.
